new_vs_preexisting_detection_mechanism.label: inspector-side tagging
new_vs_preexisting_detection_mechanism.contract: Per spec-20260503-091826 M12 / AC-12.1 iter-3 / Section 5.4 rule 3: in --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings for the listed files and tags every finding with an explicit introduced_in_diff boolean. close.md uses this tag per AC-2.6 (b) NEW-positive marker rule. An absent/null/untagged output is NOT interpreted as all-NEW; default-safe ignore applies.
new_vs_preexisting_detection_mechanism.explanation: Each finding object MUST include `introduced_in_diff: true|false`. true = the finding is a new violation introduced by this diff (eligible for NEW-positive blocking under AC-2.6); false = the finding existed in the file before this diff and is being surfaced advisorily (default-safe / non-blocking for close gate). When the inspector emits an empty findings[] array, that is an affirmative statement that no in-diff cleanliness violations were detected — not a default-safe silence.

summary.verdict_rationale: Re-verification confirms the prior CLEAN verdict. All 17 changed files live in their canonical directories with conventional names. The diff REMOVES cleanliness debt: (i) .gitignore now suppresses agent-scores.json/.lock and .claude/specs/spec-*/ telemetry leakage via root-anchored patterns; (ii) INDEX.md/README.md leak entries are removed (the INDEX.md regen also reflects the title normalization from `dot-claude` to `.claude`); (iii) hooks/doc_sync SKIP lists + tree.py PATH-AWARE _is_dot_claude_specs predicate codify ongoing suppression; (iv) test-writer agent is now fully registered across 5 harness sites (tool-policy.v1.json role definition, hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS, hooks/prompt-workflow.py agent_types, commands/dev.md for-loop, scripts/spec-check.py ALLOWED_AGENTS) — closing what would otherwise be an orphan-agent finding; (v) manifest_path semantics are consistent across agents/test-writer.md, agents/qa.md, and commands/dev.md per-task paths; (vi) all 5 python3 invocations in scripts/{score-update,score-inject,canary-verify}.sh are wrapped in venv-activating subshells; (vii) commands/dev.md decimal Sub-step 12.0/12.1 headings replaced with integer-step prose; (viii) scripts/canary-verify.sh hardcoded /tmp literals replaced with mktemp. No misplaced docs, no naming-convention violations, no temp/backup/duplicate files introduced, no dead code, no orphan agents/scripts/commands.

checklist_coverage.1_document_structure_violations: pass — INDEX.md, README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md remain in root (CLAUDE.md whitelist); docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md is in the canonical specs/ subdirectory; no .md added to project root.
checklist_coverage.2_archive_candidates: pass — no completion/plan/proposal docs introduced by this diff.
checklist_coverage.3_dev_context_cleanup: pass — no new docs/dev/ JSON artifacts authored by THIS diff's listed files; the inspection scope is limited to --changed-files.
checklist_coverage.5_duplicate_scripts: pass — scripts/canary-verify.sh, score-inject.sh, score-update.sh, spec-check.py are all canonical (no -old/-backup/.bak siblings).
checklist_coverage.6_one_time_tests: pass — diff does not introduce experimental/orphan test files.
checklist_coverage.7_non_functional_files: pass — no *.tmp, *.bak, *.swp, .DS_Store, *.pyc, __pycache__ entries in the changed-files list. The diff explicitly suppresses telemetry leakage via .gitignore + doc-sync SKIP lists.
checklist_coverage.8_orphaned_subagents: pass — agents/qa.md, agents/style-inspector.md, agents/test-writer.md are all referenced from commands/dev.md and the harness registries. test-writer was previously underwired; this diff completes registration across 5 sites.
checklist_coverage.9_unreferenced_scripts: pass — all 4 changed scripts (canary-verify.sh, score-inject.sh, score-update.sh, spec-check.py) are referenced from agents (style-inspector calls canary-verify; dev workflow invokes score-inject/score-update; subagent checkpoint contracts invoke spec-check.py).
checklist_coverage.10_orphaned_tests: pass — no test files in changed-files list.
checklist_coverage.11_historical_feature_docs: pass — the spec file docs/dev/specs/spec-20260518-225715.md is the active cycle-2 spec being implemented (not historical).
checklist_coverage.12_docs_categorization: pass — no uncategorized docs introduced in docs/ root.
checklist_coverage.13_obsolete_functionality: pass — diff modernizes legacy patterns (decimal sub-step numbering eliminated per /dev Standard 4 P3.7; hardcoded /tmp literals replaced with mktemp per P3.8). No dead env vars, unreachable branches, or superseded code paths introduced.
checklist_coverage.14_non_functional_folders: pass — no new folders introduced by this diff.
checklist_coverage.15_orphaned_commands: pass — commands/dev.md is the canonical orchestrator command (heavily referenced).
checklist_coverage.16_dead_functions: pass — hooks/doc_sync/tree.py adds _is_dot_claude_specs which is wired into the items filter (line `and not _is_dot_claude_specs(i)`); not dead. All other touched python is wiring-up edits.

preexisting_observations_non_blocking.description: Pre-existing top-level specs/ tree (separate from .claude/specs/) was not touched by this diff and is outside the changed-files scope; default-safe ignored per AC-2.6 (b).

safety_rules_compliance.no_relocate_or_delete_protected_files: satisfied — CLAUDE.md, README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md remain in project root; the diff only edits INDEX.md and README.md content (removes leaked entries), it does not relocate them.
safety_rules_compliance.no_files_modified_under_7_days_flagged_f: satisfied — empty findings[] array means nothing is flagged.
